LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Atlantic.description: When the bombs fell, the East Coast’s dense cities became tombs of glass and steel. Those who endured the fallout adapted to life in shattered high-rises and flooded metro tunnels. The Atlantic people are resourceful, tech-savvy scavengers who learned to thrive amidst rust, rain, and radiation. They speak with the echo of a thousand pre-war voices, clinging to fragments of the old world’s ambition.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Atlantic.descriptionSho: Urban survivors from the irradiated ruins of the East Coast, hardened by concrete jungles and endless storms.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Atlantic.label: Atlantic
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Cascadia.description: In the forests of old Cascadia, the sky froze before the earth burned. When nuclear winter blanketed the Northwest, small enclaves learned to live beneath the ash and snow. They are stoic, quiet survivalists—hunters, herbalists, and scavengers who built warmth from ruin. The cold sharpened them, and they speak in whispers like the wind through pine needles.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Cascadia.descriptionSho: Descendants of nuclear winter survivors from the misty ruins of the Pacific Northwest.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Cascadia.label: 卡斯卡迪亞
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Ghoul.description: Once human, now something else entirely. Ghouls are walking relics—skin sloughed, eyes hollow, voices rasping with the crackle of Geiger counters. They do not die easily, and some do not die at all. Beneath their horror lies memory, and in that memory burns an unending hunger—for the life they lost, and the world that took it.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Ghoul.descriptionShort: Humans twisted by radiation into deathless husks of their former selves.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Ghoul.label: 食屍鬼
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_NatL.description: On the Colorado Plateau, nature reclaimed what mankind lost. Small, diverse groups survived in hidden canyons, ancient ruins, and red-rock sanctuaries. The Nat’L are stewards of the wilderness, descendants of hikers, rangers, and wanderers who refused to abandon the land. They hold a quiet reverence for the world that was and the world that yet survives.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_NatL.descriptionShort: Nomadic tribes from the old National Parks, guardians of the wild places that endured the end.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_NatL.label: Nat'L
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_NuevoTexico.description: In the ashes of the Southwest, the Rio Grande became a lifeline for those who refused to die divided. Nuevo Texico rose from the union of Americans and Mexicans who shed the old borders. Their blood carries two nations, their speech blends two tongues, and their culture is a hymn to survival, sung in both sun and dust.
Descendants of the Rio Grande survivors where borders vanished and survival united all.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_NuevoTexico.label: 新德克薩斯州
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Numen.description: Numen are the green children of the wasteland—mutants born where nature refused to die. Vines lace their veins, their skin glows faintly with bioluminescent life, and their breath carries the scent of moss and decay. They are patient, serene, and unsettling, speaking softly of the forests that dream beneath the ruins.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Numen.descriptionShort: Plant-bound humans whose mutation binds flesh and flora into one.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_Numen.label: 元神
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_PureHumans.description: Pure humans are rare—descendants of vault-born bloodlines, preserved genetics, or sheer luck. They see themselves as the last unbroken line of humanity, though in a world reborn through mutation, that purity can be a curse. To some, they are relics; to others, they are hope made flesh.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_PureHumans.descriptionS: Unaffected descendants of the old world, clinging to their unmutated humanity.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_PureHumans.label: Pure Humans
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SLanter.description: FEV-mutated raccoons. Four escaped a West Tek research facility before the bombs dropped and eventually founded a hidden community. Over generations they forgot they were once raccoons, naming themselves the Tribe of the S'Lanter — meaning "Kindred."
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SLanter.label: S'Lanter
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SuperMutantFirst.descri: First generation super mutants are the earliest stable survivors of direct Forced Evolutionary Virus exposure. They are broader, harder to kill, and even further removed from baseline humanity than later generations, though their minds are just a little more preserved.
Earlier, harsher Forced Evolutionary Virus subjects, big and mean, but not mindless.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SuperMutantFirst.label: first generation super mutant
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SuperMutantSecond.descr: Super Mutants are the children of the wasteland’s cruel experiments. Towering, musclebound, and nearly immortal, they are the outcome of forced evolution and forgotten ambition. Though often seen as monsters, some retain fractured shards of who they were—flickers of humanity behind monstrous eyes.
Massive, radiation-forged brutes born of twisted science and endless war.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_SuperMutantSecond.label: second generation super mutant
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_TwoCalifornia.descripti: California burned twice—first in the blast, then in the cold. The survivors of both sides—American and Baja—bear the scars of nuclear hellfire and the ice that followed. The Two Californias forged a bitter unity, rebuilding from the bones of cities and the ghosts of surf and sand. They remember paradise, and they remember its end.
Survivors from both Californias—north and south, divided by fire yet bound by endurance.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_TwoCalifornia.label: Two California
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_VaultDweller.descriptio: Vault Dwellers grew up behind steel doors and sterile lights, sheltered from the chaos above. When the world outside decayed, they evolved within—disciplined, educated, but naive to the raw savagery of the surface. Some see them as saviors. Others, as pampered relics playing hero in a world that forgot their comfort.
Descendants of those who survived the Great War sealed within underground vaults.
LanguageData.WestTek_Xenotype_VaultDweller.label: Vault Dweller
LanguageData.Baseliner.description: 廢土人是後核世界的支柱。他們既不是變種人，也不是地窖出生的人，而是拾荒者、商人、掠奪者和定居者，他們以地球上的開放傷痕為家。每一個傷疤都講述一個故事，每一個瘀青都是一個教訓。它們是人類能夠在任何情況下生存下來的活生生的證明——甚至包括它自己。
LanguageData.Baseliner.label: 廢土人
LanguageData.Sanguophage.description: 曾經是人類，現在受到飢餓和寒冷的詛咒，溫迪戈是舊世界最黑暗角落的吸血鬼殘餘。他們的身體憔悴，皮膚蒼白如霜，眼睛裡燃燒著野性的飢餓。他們以血和肉為食，並不是自願的，而是透過某種古老而可怕的東西的低語，這種東西不會讓他們死亡。
LanguageData.Sanguophage.descriptionShort: 夜間的掠食者，以活人的肉體為食，以防止腐爛。
LanguageData.Sanguophage.label: 溫迪戈
